contract_conformance.notes: All five lanes conformed to contracts/w29-remediation-contract.md. Verified each fix at its cited site against docs/REMAINING-WORK.md W29 rows: EM-272 rejects the +1-teacher no-op via (ok=False) so teach_skill_event records NO skill_taught contribution; EM-275 derives loop._last_building_round from world.round (fresh world still 0); EM-276 gates the trial effect on defendant.alive; EM-277 floors the launder fee at max(1,...); EM-278 returns early with master_plan_dropped when a morph is already active; EM-279 holds the belief FIFO cap at consolidate_at+1 so the sleep-sweep is reachable; EM-288 (filed PLAUSIBLE) was confirmed REAL and serialized as a sorted nested {aid:{skill:xp}} additive key with an empty-ledger fallback; EM-289 records project_built on the co_build finisher; EM-280/285/290 are compact prompt-surface fixes (no standing LLM call, no throttle); EM-281 bases the retry budget on the actual attempt-1 tokens; EM-295 memoizes planar_faces on a structural node/edge signature (self-invalidates on the direct graph splice world.step_master_plan_morph performs); EM-273/282/283/284 derive streets/labels/lots/pedestrian-ids from graph EDGES with a grid fallback and crossing bail; EM-274/286/291/292/293/294 fix the cache leak, memo identity, GPU dispose, rAF getComputedStyle, tab a11y and diary fallback; EM-287/296 size+magic validate every provider return site and SSRF-guard the url fetch. Determinism law honored: new serialized fields (_skill_xp) carry old-snapshot fallbacks; the _faces_cache lives only in the instance __dict__ and is never enumerated by CityGraph.to_dict. Test-file discipline honored — every lane added NEW test files only; no existing source or test outside ownership was edited (test_imagegen_providers.py's one edit is the sanctioned SSRF-stub adaptation the providers lane owns).

coverage.notes: All 25 findings (EM-272..EM-296) have a dedicated regression test file (8 sim-core, 5 runtime, 4 city-layout, 6 frontend-ui, 2 providers). Spot-checked 8 by reasoning from the test body that they FAIL on pre-fix code and assert the user-visible SYMPTOM, not just the mechanism: EM-272 (contributions.skill_taught stays 0 + score 0), EM-275 (notoriety unchanged on resume; a teeth-test pins the pre-fix 48), EM-281 (retry 16384 > boosted 4096), EM-286 (content sig stable across distinct refs, flips on car_policy/edge-set/per-edge override), EM-274 (LRU eviction yields a new ref with identical content), EM-288 (resumed run levels on the SAME grant as continuous), EM-287 (non-image/oversized b64 rejected), EM-296 (metadata host never fetched). The remaining 17 were assertion-scanned and are all symptom-level (jailing/confiscation, launder price, master_plan_dropped, memory event fired, project_built score, prompt-menu contents, constitution cap+dedup, funded-project gating, grid-plat non-empty, whole-graph [] on crossings, disposeSpy calls, getComputedStyle count, ARIA attributes, diary non-blank). Added 20 QE cross-lane probe tests (backend/tests/test_w29_qe_probes.py = 16; web/src/components/world3d/w29qe.crossmorph.test.ts = 4) covering the seams no single lane owned. Minor (non-blocking): EM-286 asserts the trafficGraphSig contract rather than the React memo hit-rate directly — acceptable since the signature IS the fix and its content-vs-identity behavior is the symptom.

security.notes: EM-287/296 verified END-TO-END into the loop write site (loop._spawn_image_fetch writes ONLY _image_provider.fetch_png() bytes, and all five provider return sites — Freellmapi b64+url, Pollinations, Gemini, Cloudflare — pass through _valid_image; image_id is machine-derived img_+10hex so no prompt/id path traversal). Probe 5 could NOT get a non-image (HTML/SVG stored-XSS shape) or oversized payload past the guard via the URL shape, nor an SSRF target through a multi-hop public->public->private(169.254.169.254) redirect chain (each hop re-validated at the loop top BEFORE fetch; httpx auto-redirects disabled). The loop write-site drop-bad/keep-good composition test passes on a tmp assets dir (no repo artifacts). Documented residuals (lane-acknowledged, low severity for the threat model of a compromised upstream free-image provider, all off the critical path behind the fetch semaphore + 12s timeout): (a) the body is buffered into resp.content before the size cap is checked — a memory-pressure vector, not a write/serve vector; (b) DNS-rebinding TOCTOU (the guard resolves the host, then httpx resolves again on GET). Neither is a bypass of the write/serve guard; both are follow-ups, not blockers.

regression.notes: Full backend suite 1912 passed (1896 wave-gate baseline + my 16 probes) in ~51s; full frontend 1210 passed across 103 files (1206 baseline + my 4 probes); tsc -b --force rc=0 (my new .test.ts typechecks). No existing assertion perturbed; no side-artifacts leaked into data/assets/images (probe 5 writes only under pytest tmp_path). Determinism control in probe 1 confirms two independent continuous runs produce byte-identical snapshots, and the resumed run is byte-identical to the continuous run at every post-fork round with BOTH _skill_xp (EM-288) and a decaying notoriety (EM-275) live in the snapshot; the teeth-test proves the probe would catch the pre-fix divergence.

test_results.backend: 1912 passed in ~51s (.venv/bin/python -m pytest backend/tests -q). Includes 16 new cross-lane probes in backend/tests/test_w29_qe_probes.py (probes 1,2,4,5). 0 failures.
test_results.frontend: 1210 passed across 103 files (/usr/local/bin/npx vitest run), including 4 new probes in web/src/components/world3d/w29qe.crossmorph.test.ts (probe 3). /usr/local/bin/npx tsc -b --force rc=0.

issues: Non-blocking (follow-up, visual-only): EM-273's 'cars sweep the full ±span' clause is only PARTIALLY resolved. The fix ties street EXISTENCE and street LABELS to real axis-aligned edges (labels clipped to the paved min/max), but ambient CARS still sweep the GLOBAL trafficSpan(streets) — CityStreet carries no paved extent and carOffset ranges over ±trafficSpan regardless of a line's actual road length. So a PARTIALLY demolished/thinned line keeps a car that visually overshoots into its now-roadless portion. Repro: web/src/components/world3d/w29qe.crossmorph.test.ts::'CHARACTERIZATION: labels clip to the paved span but the car sweep does not'. Not gate-blocking: traffic is purely visual (never in the sim/replay/determinism surface, per trafficLayout.ts), and the finding's PRIMARY symptoms — phantom NAMED streets and cars on FULLY-roadless lines after demolish/thin/morph — ARE resolved (probe 3 confirms zero cars + zero phantom streets under a city-wide ban on a mid-morph graph, and a fully-demolished line drops both its street and its car).

Non-blocking (doc drift): backend/petridish/engine/world.py grant_skill_xp docstring (~line 6536) still states the _skill_xp ledger is 'NOT snapshotted ... restarts the partial-xp clock at 0, a documented, harmless rounding' — directly contradicted by EM-288, which now serializes it (the to_snapshot comment even notes 'the old harmless-rounding note was wrong'). Documentation-only; behavior is correct and tested.
Observation (no action): coordination/qa-report.json previously held the Wave I report; overwritten here with the W29 gate report (qe-agent owns this file per contract Wave 2).

gate_decision.reason: All 25 W29 findings (EM-272..EM-296) are fixed at their cited sites with symptom-level regression tests that fail on pre-fix code; no fix relocates rather than resolves its finding. Adversarial cross-lane probes all returned CONFIRMED-OK: EM-155 fork/replay is byte-identical with both the new _skill_xp ledger (EM-288) and live crime-decay (EM-275) in the snapshot; the EM-295 faces cache self-invalidates under the direct morph splice and never leaks into serialization; a mid-morph city-wide car ban yields zero cars AND zero phantom streets (EM-273×284); the +1 teach no-op farms zero contribution and the failure reaches the agent (EM-272); and no non-image/oversized/SSRF payload reaches the .png disk writer (EM-287/296). Full suites green with the probes present (backend 1912, frontend 1210, tsc 0). The two open items are non-blocking follow-ups (a visual-only ambient-car overshoot on partially-thinned lines; a stale docstring) with concrete repros/pointers — no unfixed critical, no HIGH blocker, all scores >= 3.
